{"query":"TSMC Arizona fab construction progress 2026 3nm 2nm production start date employment number","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1896,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.tsmc.com/english","title":"Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited","content":"TSMC publishes its Sustainability Report annually to transparently reflect the values and progress the Company delivers."},{"url":"https://tech-insider.org/tsmc-arizona-165-billion-expansion-gigafab-2026/","title":"TSMC's $165B Arizona GigaFab: Reshaping US Chips [2026]","content":"Looking ahead, TSMC has already announced the N2P enhancement and the A16 (1.6nm) node with backside power delivery, both targeted for H2 2026.
TSMC currently employs over 3,000 workers at its Arizona site, but the full build-out could require tens of thousands of additional employees across engineering, operations, and construction roles.
The A16 (1.6nm) process could follow within 12–18 months of the 2nm ramp.
The second fab will manufacture 3nm chips starting in 2027, and the third fab is expected to produce 2nm chips by 2028–2029.
Samsung’s 2nm process targets only 21,000 wafers per month by end of 2026, while Intel’s competing 18A node remains in development.
The CHIPS and Science Act provided initial subsidies estimated between $6.6–11.6 billion in direct grants to support TSMC’s first three Arizona fabs.
Equipment installation is planned for Q3 2026 (July–September), with high-volume 3nm chip production targeted for 2027.
TSMC reported $122.4 billion in total revenue for 2025 (up 36% year-over-year) and guided Q1 2026 revenue of $34.6–35.8 billion."},{"url":"https://ro.careers.tsmc.com/job/Phoenix-2026-Corporate-Planning-Engineer-AZ-85083/1331901566/","title":"2026 Corporate Planning Engineer Job Details | TSMC","content":"TSMC Arizona’s first fab will operate its leading-edge semiconductor process technology (N4 process), starting production in the first half of 2025.
The recently announced third fab will manufacture chips using 2nm or even more advanced process technology, with production starting by the end of the decade."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TSMC_Arizona","title":"TSMC Arizona - Wikipedia","content":"As of 2025, the first fab has been completed and is producing four-nanometer (nm) chips.
On December 6, 2022 an opening ceremony was held for the first plant, attended by US President Joe Biden along with Morris Chang (TSMC), Tim Cook (Apple Inc.), Jensen Huang (Nvidia), and Sanjay Mehrotra (Micron Technology).
TSMC originally targeted production for 2028, but announced in January 2026 that volume production of 3 nm chips at the second fab would start in the second half of 2027, earlier than previously projected.

Fab 21's Phase 2 and Phase 3 … completion slated for 2025 and 2027, respectively. … nanosheet transistor structure for TSMC's 2nm process"},{"url":"https://www.aztechcouncil.org/tsmc-moves-up-production-second-fab-html/","title":"TSMC accelerates production timeline for new Arizona factory, reports say - Arizona Technology Council","content":"Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. plans to install chipmaking equipment in its second Arizona plant next summer as it prepares for mass production at the fab a year earlier than projected, according to an Asian media report.
The company completed construction of its second fab — which will utilize 3-nanometer process technology — in April and broke ground on its third fab at its north Phoenix site that same month.
TSMC’s third fab is expected to utilize 2-nanometer and A16 process technologies, while its fourth fab will utilize N2 and A16 process technologies."}]}

The U.S. Department of Commerce awarded TSMC Arizona up to $6.6 billion in direct funding under the CHIPS and Science Act. This funding will support TSMC’s investment of more than $65 billion in three greenfield leading-edge fabs in Phoenix, Arizona, which will manufacture the world’s most advanced semiconductors. With this funding, TSMC Arizona plans to construct a scaled leading-edge cluster in Arizona, bringing some of the most-advanced process technologies to the United States and creating approximately 6,000 direct manufacturing jobs, more than 20,000 total unique construction jobs, and tens of thousands of indirect jobs in this decade.
